name: Waialae Nui Valley
desc: Valley floor below Waialae Nui Ridge — more affordable than the ridge above, family-friendly, lush and quiet.
insider: You get the prestigious Waialae Nui address without the ridge-top prices — a smart value play in East Honolulu
The valley floor is sheltered from trade winds — noticeably calmer and warmer than the exposed ridge above
Walking access to Kahala Mall and Waialae Avenue restaurants is 10-15 minutes — close to Kaimuki's food scene too
The community is quieter and more family-oriented than flashier Kahala next door — lots of multigenerational families
Homes range from modest 1960s ranches to renovated contemporary builds — $1M to $2M depending on condition and lot size
The valley is lush with mature tropical landscaping — mango, avocado, and plumeria trees are everywhere
The only way in and out is via Waialae Nui Ridge Road — zero through traffic keeps the streets very quiet
Kahala Elementary School serves the area and is one of the higher-rated public elementary schools in Honolulu
The valley floor can be humid and still on windless days — mosquitoes thrive here, especially after rain
Close enough to Kaimuki restaurants on Waialae Ave for a quick drive — 12th Ave Grill and Mud Hen Water are 10 minutes away
